_comment: Mutation testing measures TEST STRENGTH (do our tests actually catch bugs?) — the rigorous metric line coverage can't give, and which esbuild tree-shaking hides. Command runner against our esbuild unit harness; the command runs ONLY the test files that cover the mutated sources (fast). Scoped to small, pure, heavily-tested logic; widen `mutate` for a deeper nightly pass. Run: npm run test:mutation

commandRunner.command: node test/run-unit.mjs test/kernel/reveal.test.ts test/kernel/ranges.test.ts test/kernel/outline.test.ts test/kernel/hostAdapters.test.ts test/kernel/decorate.test.ts test/vault/linkParser.test.ts test/vault/vaultIndex.test.ts test/shared/settings.test.ts test/vault/newNote.test.ts

_mutate_comment: Scoped to small, pure, heavily-tested logic where survived mutants mean weak tests. Newer pure modules `settings` + `newNote` are included. Deliberately EXCLUDED (covered by 100% line coverage + L3 e2e instead): `editActions.ts` / `autocomplete.ts` / `folding.ts` (their pure helpers sit next to CM6 glue the Node harness can't exercise) and `attachment.ts` (a MIME/extension data table whose entries read as equivalent mutants). Widen for a deeper nightly pass.
